Thunderstorms Across Delhi NCR, Mumbai, Chennai, Karnataka, Rajasthan, Jammu, Hyderabad & More — Full City-Wise Forecast - The Sunday Guardian,https://news.google.?oc=5,The Sunday Guardian,2026-04-15T01:21:53+05:30,en,Jammu and Kashmir,Jammu,"(""heat advisory"" OR ""heat action plan"" OR ""heat alert"" OR ""IMD warning"" OR ""IMD alert"" OR ""red alert"" OR ""orange alert"" OR ""yellow alert"" OR ""NDMA advisory"" OR ""cooling shelter"" OR ""relief measures"" OR ""heat compensation"" OR ""severe weather warning"") Jammu and Kashmir","Weather Today, 15 April 2026: India is witnessing a rapid shift toward peak summer conditions as temperatures climb sharply across northern, central and southern regions. According to the India Meteorological Department, nearly 95% of the country is experiencing clear skies, accelerating daytime heating. Several cities are already approaching 40°C, while central and peninsular regions may see temperatures between 42°C and 45°C this week with warmer nights, falling humidity and fading western disturbances are further intensifying heat stress, particularly across urban centers.
Will it Rain or Snow in the Hills?

Light snowfall remains possible in higher reaches of Jammu and Kashmir and Himachal Pradesh, especially in Srinagar and Gulmarg however, rainfall across plains remains limited with dry and hot conditions dominating most of India.
Why Is The Heat Building Up So Fast Across India?
